T0462.title: Pig Butchering Scam
T0462.definition: A sophisticated long-term fraud scheme where scammers build romantic or friendship relationships with victims over weeks or months before convincing them to invest in fake cryptocurrency or investment platforms.
T0462.description: Pig butchering scams represent an evolution of romance scams combined with investment fraud, named after the practice of fattening a pig before slaughter. Scammers initiate contact through dating apps, social media, or wrong number messages, then invest weeks or months building trust and emotional connection with victims. Once rapport is established, scammers gradually introduce investment opportunities, typically fake cryptocurrency trading platforms or foreign exchange schemes. Victims are initially allowed to withdraw small profits to build confidence, then encouraged to invest larger sums. When victims attempt to withdraw substantial amounts, they encounter fabricated fees, taxes, or account freezes. Scammers often operate from organized compounds in Southeast Asia where trafficked workers are forced to execute these schemes under coercion. The combination of emotional manipulation and fake financial gains makes these scams devastatingly effective, with individual losses often exceeding hundreds of thousands of dollars.
T0462.usageExample: A victim lost $450,000 in a pig butchering scam after a months-long online relationship led them to invest life savings into a fake cryptocurrency platform that showed fabricated returns.
